ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken wing food product class; no specific orange-glazed variant exists. closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cooked chicken wings broadly; no orange-glaze specific code exists. closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for orange-glazed chicken wings as a food product at this specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of French Orange-Glazed Chicken Wings consists of approximately 4 to 5 wings, weighing around 200 g, served as a main dish or sharing plate.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken wing, cooked, glazed — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05303 / adapted composite with orange glaze)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base values derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked chicken wings (skin on, roasted). Orange glaze contribution (sugar, orange juice, soy sauce) estimated using USDA FoodData Central component data for orange juice and sugar to adjust carbohydrate and sugar values upward. Sodium adjusted for soy sauce contribution. This is a composite estimate; no exact entry for French orange-glazed chicken wings exists in any approved primary regional source. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed meat-and-glaze dishes — analogous estimate based on glazed chicken (orange/honey glaze) from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for French orange-glazed chicken wings. GI estimated at 45 based on analogous glazed poultry dishes with moderate sugar-based sauces. The dominant macronutrient is protein and fat; the orange glaze contributes moderate simple sugars (sucrose, fructose from orange juice) which have a GI of approximately 50–65, but the overall dish GI is moderated by the high protein and fat content. Composite GI estimated at 45 (low). GL per 200 g serving = (45 × 9.8 × 2.0) / 100 = 8.8. Source: University of Sydney GI Database analogous category; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2008.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy sauce in glaze
hypertension.guidance: Each 200 g serving provides approximately 840 mg sodium, representing about 37% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; patients should limit portion size and request reduced-sodium preparation where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugars in orange glaze contributing to glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the estimated GI is low (45) and the glycemic load per serving is moderate (8.8), the orange glaze contains added sugars and fruit-derived simple carbohydrates; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and sodium from skin-on chicken wings and soy-based glaze
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Skin-on chicken wings contribute saturated fat (approximately 7.8 g per 200 g serving) and the soy-based glaze adds significant sodium; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and consider skinless preparation to reduce saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: French Orange-Glazed Chicken Wings is a Western-inspired poultry dish featuring chicken wings coated in a sweet-tangy orange glaze made from orange juice, orange zest, sugar or honey, soy sauce, garlic, and butter, then roasted or baked until caramelised and sticky. The dish draws on classic French culinary technique — specifically the use of a citrus reduction glaze (reminiscent of canard à l'orange) applied to chicken wings, a format popularised in café and bistro settings across Southeast Asia, including Singapore and Malaysia. The wings are typically served as a sharing appetiser or main course. Texturally, the exterior is lacquered and slightly crisp from caramelisation, while the interior remains juicy. The flavour profile is sweet, citrusy, and savoury with a mild umami depth from the soy component. Nutritionally, the dish is protein-rich (approximately 20.5 g per 100 g) and moderate in fat (14.2 g per 100 g), with the glaze contributing meaningful added sugars (7.5 g per 100 g) and sodium (420 mg per 100 g). The glycemic index is estimated at 45 (low), moderated by the high protein and fat content despite the sugar-containing glaze. Clinically, the dish is of moderate concern for hypertension due to sodium from soy sauce, and for cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat from skin-on wings. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, the moderate glycemic load (8.8 per serving) is manageable with appropriate portion control. The dish is halal-compatible when prepared with halal-certified soy sauce and chicken.
common_user_queries: How many calories are in orange glazed chicken wings?
Are French orange glazed chicken wings healthy?
What is the GI of glazed chicken wings?
Can diabetics eat orange glazed chicken wings?
How much sodium is in orange chicken wings?
What are the macros for chicken wings with orange sauce?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for French orange-glazed chicken wings exists in any approved primary regional source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cooked chicken wing data with composite estimation for the orange glaze contribution. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category for glazed poultry. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are a composite estimate: base chicken wing values from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (roasted, skin on), with carbohydrate, sugar, and sodium adjusted upward to account for the orange glaze (orange juice, sugar/honey, soy sauce). 2. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. 3. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, and all B-vitamins are estimated from USDA SR Legacy chicken wing data and are approximate. 4. The cuisine tag 'Malaysian' in the input reflects the restaurant context (EC Coffee Bar, Carpenter Street, Singapore); the dish itself is French-inspired. Both contexts are preserved. 5. Price data (SGD 26.30) is preserved from the field survey source record for reference only and is not used as a nutritional evidence source. 6. Halal status is assumed based on typical café preparation in Singapore; formal halal certification status of the specific restaurant is not confirmed. 7. All -1 sentinel values in per_100g have been replaced with estimated numeric values from USDA FoodData Central where available; no -1 values remain in this record.

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list — all entries confirmed present verbatim. Verified GI category threshold (45 ≤ 55 = low). Confirmed no -1 sentinel values remain undocumented. Confirmed no zero core macros. Confirmed ontology match_type values are only exact/closest_parent/none.
